anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: OCD, scrupulosity, reassurance seeking, abusive authority, and valid solitude.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in any public practice unless the codebook includes clinical exclusions, unsafe-authority detection, anti-reassurance rules, and a valid-solitude option.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: The model says a compulsive self-scorer may need a teacher, peer, clinician, or rule to hold the check. In OCD or scrupulosity, the same transfer can become reassurance compulsion. In abusive communities, external holding becomes control. In valid solitude, outside holding can weaken needed self-trust.

claim_units: A humility, release, or return check can become harmful self-scoring.
Failure mode should determine check holder.
Bypassers need conduct accountability more than relief from checking.
Compulsive self-scorers may need the diagnostic held outside the self or suspended.
Oscillators require present-state routing, not fixed type assignment.
The practice is only publishable for a clear cohort: stable high-achievement practitioners harmed by self-scoring.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: High-performing workers whose worth is tied to quality scores will complete more and ruminate less when acceptance criteria or reviewers hold the check, while avoidant workers will need a self-held concrete completion check.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: The post-practice check becomes the post-output quality diagnostic. Depending on failure mode, quality can be held by the worker, a reviewer, acceptance criteria, a release rule, or temporarily not checked.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Generic code review, WIP limits, or ordinary management explain all outcome differences, or holder assignment by failure mode adds no predictive value.

exact_claim: Post-practice checks are not automatically safe. The decisive safety variable is who holds the check today: the practitioner, another person, a rule, ordinary conduct, or no check. Bypassers may need a self-held conduct check, compulsive self-scorers may need external, rule-held, or suspended checking, and oscillators need state-based assignment rather than identity labels.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Compared with external or rule-held conditions, self-held checks increase rumination, shame, comparison, and repeated interpretation, while not improving next-day conduct.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Stable high-achievement practitioners high in achievement-contingent self-worth will fare worse with self-held after-use checks than with externally held, rule-held, or suspended checks.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Two-week randomized pilot using contingencies of self-worth, rumination scales, mood ratings, and concrete duty completion.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Self-held checks reduce rumination and improve conduct as well as or better than external, rule-held, or suspended checks.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: In high-bypass, low-self-scoring participants, a single conduct check increases repair behavior and reduces avoidance without increasing shame.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practitioners using insight to avoid responsibility need a self-held return-to-conduct check more than they need rest from checking.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Screened practice pilot with spiritual bypassing, avoidance, self-worth contingency, and next-day conduct measures.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The conduct check does not improve behavior, or it increases self-scoring at the same rate as in the compulsive scorer group.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Split-source coding of manuals predicts held-out warnings and repairs involving self, teacher, community, rule, conduct, or no-check options.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practice manuals and teachers should assign diagnostic custody differently by stage, risk, and support ecology.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind manual coding plus practitioner interviews using concrete misinterpretation and repair cases.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: All holder assignments are explained by institution type, teacher personality, or generic spiritual direction without added predictive gain from failure-mode coding.

literature_search_queries: exact structural near neighbor same reflective diagnostic different holder by failure mode spiritual direction

Pargament religion problem solving self directing deferring collaborative coping
Varieties of Contemplative Experience support structures appraisal social context meditation challenges
OCD scrupulosity reassurance seeking compulsion ERP external reassurance
self focused attention negative affect meta-analysis rumination depression anxiety
spiritual bypassing Welwood Trungpa practice ego avoidance accountability

near_neighbors.difference: This candidate changes the public line and practice wording, but does not add a new structural variable beyond the gate-holder model.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major downgrade. Treat as a renamed sibling unless separability testing proves a different prediction.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close. It already states the three-gate structure: result-language permission, current bypassing versus self-scoring risk, and diagnostic custody.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary record: Who Holds The Gate, supplied in recent originality audits.

near_neighbors.difference: Ignatius does not frame the issue through achievement-contingent self-worth, self-scoring, no-check practice, or clinical rumination risk.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the core mechanism to known spiritual direction territory. The possible contribution is a modern safety-routing codebook, not a discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for holder assignment. The exercitant reports movements, the director adapts exercises, secrecy is broken by disclosure, and the director is limited from over-directing the final choice.
near_neighbors.source: Ignatius of Loyola, Spiritual Exercises, Annotations 7, 15, 17, Rule 13, and scruple notes, https://www.orderofthecommonlife.org/spiritual-exercises-full-text

near_neighbors.difference: It concerns coping with God and agency, not post-practice diagnostics, result-language checks, or self-worth scoring.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Reduces novelty for custody language, leaves room only for a narrower practice-safety extension.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for support locus and agency distribution: self-directing, deferring, and collaborative styles already sort who is experienced as carrying problem-solving responsibility.
near_neighbors.source: Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process: Three Styles of Coping, 1988, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: VCE does not offer the exact holder-routing rule for after-use checks in high-achievement self-scorers.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the safety and support parts to established contemplative-adverse-effects territory.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for meditation-related challenges, appraisal, differential support structures, and the role of social context in interpreting practice effects.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., The Varieties of Contemplative Experience, PLOS ONE 2017, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

near_neighbors.difference: It is not about spiritual practice completion or assigning diagnostic custody.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Supports the caution but does not make the model original.
near_neighbors.overlap: Supports the risk claim that private self-focus can correlate with depression, generalized anxiety, and negative affect.
near_neighbors.source: Mor and Winquist, Self-Focused Attention and Negative Affect, Psychological Bulletin 2002, DOI 10.1037/0033-2909.128.4.638, indexed at https://www.scirp.org/reference/referencespapers?referenceid=1457149

near_neighbors.difference: The clinical literature is not about contemplative after-use checks, but it directly constrains any external-holder instruction.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Forces a clinical exclusion pathway and lowers practice-testability for scrupulosity and OCD-like cases.
near_neighbors.overlap: Important anomaly and near-neighbor. External reassurance and repeated checking can maintain OCD, while ERP requires resisting rituals rather than transferring them to another holder.
near_neighbors.source: Reassurance seeking in OCD and anxiety disorders, for example https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/behavioural-and-cognitive-psychotherapy/article/abs/patterns-of-reassurance-seeking-and-reassurancerelated-behaviours-in-ocd-and-anxiety-disorders/34C1F20E285643D8D7FAD482052F3EA7 and Stanford ERP summary https://med.stanford.edu/ocd/treatment/psychotherapy.html

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a standalone discovery. Cross-index or merge with Who Holds The Gate, Keep What Can Correct You, The Test Is How You Return, and correction-holder records.
Build a pre-registered holder-routing codebook with fields for failure mode, current state, check holder, unsafe holder, no-check option, clinical exclusion, warning, repair, and evidence passage.
Use split-source coding. Do not let one passage establish holder assignment and confirm the predicted repair.
Add mandatory anomaly values for OCD reassurance seeking, scrupulosity, dependency, abusive authority, valid solitude, no-product practice, no-result rest, clinician-held care, and undecidable.
Pilot only with screening for rumination, perfectionism, OCD and scrupulosity, trauma, depression, mania, dissociation, addiction withdrawal, authority harm, avoidance, and workload coercion.
Interview practitioners for concrete mischeck and repair cases, not agreement with the slogan.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.57 to 0.28. The core is already present in the supplied Who Holds The Gate audit and has close external neighbors in Ignatian direction, spiritual direction, Pargament agency styles, contemplative adverse-effects support, ERP anti-reassurance rules, and self-focused-attention research. Human-condition fit is real but bounded: it serves stable high-achievement practitioners, founders, students, creators, caregivers, and self-directed meditators who convert insight into worth evidence. It is not for acute crisis, mania, psychosis, severe depression, dissociation, addiction withdrawal, active OCD or scrupulosity reassurance loops, abusive authority settings, or people whose main need is restored self-trust.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is ordinary spiritual direction, sponsorship, supervision, ERP triage, or contemplative-adverse-effects support, and that the Lumenary framing adds no new prediction or repair rule.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the holder-routing question to predict failures they previously missed, especially self-held humility checks worsening self-worth scoring, or external checks becoming reassurance loops.
questions: When a practitioner misuses a reflection or insight, how do you decide whether the next check should be self-held, guide-held, rule-held, conduct-held, or suspended?
In actual failure cases, did outside guidance reduce harm, or did it become reassurance seeking, dependency, approval hunger, or spiritual abuse?
Is the bypasser versus self-scorer distinction already native to your practice, or does it change routing decisions?
For high-achievement practitioners, what observable sign tells you a humility check has become another score?
Which cases should receive no practice, no check, or clinical referral?
relevant_practitioners: Buddhist meditation teachers familiar with adverse practice effects
ERP clinicians treating OCD and scrupulosity
Therapists working with perfectionism and burnout

primary_pair: Ignatius of Loyola, Spiritual Exercises, Annotations 7, 15, 17, Rule 13, and scruple notes 348-350, https://www.orderofthecommonlife.org/spiritual-exercises-full-text

MN 22 Alagaddupama Sutta, Heart Sutra, Shinran On Jinen-Honi, and Dogen Bendowa or practice-realization sources, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/MN/MN22.html, https://www.lotsawahouse.org/words-of-the-buddha/heart-sutra, https://shinranworks.com/letters/lamp-for-the-latter-ages/on-jinen-honi-1258/, https://buddhismnow.com/2023/10/08/practice-within-realisation-dogen/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. External holding can be wise guidance, ordinary sponsorship, clinical triage, coercive authority, approval dependence, or OCD reassurance. No-check can mean healthy rest, avoidance, depression drift, or Dogen-style non-instrumentality. The bypasser, scorer, and oscillator labels are too crude without validated measures and concrete behavior markers.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Still needs full reading of Ignatian directorial manuals, contemporary spiritual direction ethics, AA sponsorship sources, ERP and scrupulosity treatment literature, VCE person-centered support, spiritual bypassing literature, and achievement-contingent self-worth studies before practice publication.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Ignatius is the closest primary-text neighbor. The director adapts exercises to the exercitant, receives reports of movements and thoughts, reveals dangerous secrecy to a confessor or spiritual person, and is also restrained from seizing the exercitant's final choice. The scruple notes already distinguish useful sensitivity from harmful over-fineness and counsel a mean. MN 22 adds that the raft must be grasped correctly before release, so release is not a generic self-check. The Heart Sutra denies attainment while retaining reliance on Prajnaparamita. Shinran and Dogen strain the whole model because no-calculation and practice-realization refuse a clean separable product that the practitioner can audit.

title: Not Every Check Should Be Yours
unlike_statement: Unlike Ignatian direction, Pargament agency styles, VCE support mapping, ERP anti-reassurance rules, and the prior Lumenary Who Holds The Gate record, this candidate says that a post-practice check must first ask who should hold the diagnostic today, because the same check can repair bypassing and worsen achievement-contingent self-scoring. That distinction is useful, but it is mostly a renamed and narrower safety-routing heuristic.
